Details:
Two studies presenting TCE concentrations typical of those seen in drinking water applications found that UV irradiation alone was somewhat ineffective at reducing TCE. With UV exposure times of 18 to 300 seconds, removals of 18 to 21 percent were achieved. It was found that increasing UV exposure time did not significantly improve TCE reduction. However, the addition of TiO2 significantly increased the effectiveness of UV treatment to 90% removal.
For TCE concentrations higher than those typically seen in drinking water applications, the use of UV treatment resulted in removal efficiencies of 43 to 84 percent, depending on the water type and UV intensity. The use of TiO2 in combination with UV achieved 35 to 98 percent removal in three bench scale studies. Increased removal was achieved with increased UV intensity.
